summary: Frontend-only review of the Services index + ServiceDetail redesign (3D animated ServiceArt SVGs replacing stock ). Verified public hero, 8 service cards, Infographic stat badges, CTA, 4 service detail pages (ai-driven-development, app-development, saas-development, app-maintenance-support), process timeline, related-services SVG thumbnails. Ran a quick admin-login regression across landing, dashboard, intern-hub (/app/manager), badges, and chat — all pages load and render data-testids with no console/page errors.

issues: Tag text reads e.g. 'warm tier' / 'cool tier' (uses svc.tint) rather than the friendlier 'premium tier' string mentioned in the requirements. Not blocking — 'may be present' per spec — but the copy currently surfaces an internal style token to the user. Consider hard-coding 'premium tier' or mapping tints to friendlier labels.

verification_results.services_hero_headline: Engineered end-to-end for outcomes that ship. (matches)

action_items: (Optional polish) ServiceDetail floating tag currently outputs '{svc.tint} tier' (e.g. 'warm tier'). Either hard-code 'premium tier' as per the spec or map tints → human labels.
critical_code_review_comments: ServiceDetail.jsx has 500 lines and combines hero, capabilities, tech, process, deliverables, FAQ, related sections. Could be split into smaller subcomponents for maintainability.
Services.jsx uses index for data-testid (`service-card-${index}`). Functional but slug-based testids (`service-card-${svc.slug}`) would be more stable across reorderings.

ServiceArt.jsx SVG <defs> uses shared id ('deck', 'sh') — fine for now, but if multiple instances are rendered on the same page (they are, both in Services and ServiceDetail related cards), the duplicate ids can cause subtle filter/gradient leakage between instances in some browsers. Consider per-instance unique ids.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public /services and 4 /services/ pages render the new animated ServiceArt SVGs with NO stock tags in the hero. All 8 slugs link correctly. Infographic stat badges present (cross + saas variants). Regression of authed routes (admin) passed. Only cosmetic note: floating tag literal 'premium tier' is currently ' tier' due to `${svc.tint || 'premium'} tier` always falling through to a truthy tint.
